signal.title: Russian spring offensive stalling as internal critics admit Ukrainian counterattacks 'rather successful'; Kremlin halts gasoline exports; Zelensky proposes Easter ceasefire — dismissed by Moscow
signal.body: ISW reports Russian milbloggers and a Duma Defence Committee deputy now openly questioning Russia's ability to achieve its 2026 offensive objectives. Ukraine's long-range strike campaign has forced Russia to halt all gasoline exports from 1 April. Zelensky proposed an Easter ceasefire (16 April) — Kremlin dismissed it immediately. Hungary election enters final 12 days with Tisza leading Fidesz by 7-23 points depending on pollster. SAFE second wave: €17.1B proposed for France and Czechia.

defence_developments.headline: SAFE Second Wave: €17.1B Proposed for France and Czechia
defence_developments.summary: European Commission proposed €15.09B for France and €2.06B for Czechia under SAFE defence loan programme. Council has 4 weeks to approve. First payments for initial 8 countries (€22.5B tranche) expected from April 2026. Total programme: €150B.

defence_developments.headline: European Council Reaffirms Defence Readiness Ramp-Up by 2030
defence_developments.summary: 19-20 March European Council conclusions: leaders reaffirmed determination to 'decisively ramp up Europe's defence readiness by 2030.' €194.9B total EU support to Ukraine confirmed. New €90B package for 2026-27 prepared but loan blocked by Orbán. 20th sanctions package targeting Russia's energy revenues prepared.

hybrid_threats.headline: Russia Gasoline Export Halt Confirms Ukrainian Strike Campaign Impact
hybrid_threats.summary: Russia halting all gasoline exports 1 April - 31 July 2026 to stabilise domestic fuel prices. Directive from Deputy PM Novak. Ukrainian drone strikes have caused estimated 40% loss in Russia's oil export capacity this month (Reuters). ~5M metric tons / 117K bpd normally exported.

hybrid_threats.headline: Czech Pardubice: Intelligence Probes Russian False-Flag Scenario
hybrid_threats.summary: Czech intelligence (NCTEKK) investigating whether 'Earthquake Faction' is a front for foreign intelligence operation targeting Ukrainian defence production on NATO soil. 7 suspects total sought. One suspect shouted 'Free Palestine' at court. Group threatened to publish LPP documents by 20 April. LPP denies Elbit cooperation. Production relocated to Pardubice after 2022 Russian invasion.

hybrid_threats.headline: Zelensky Proposes Easter Ceasefire — Kremlin Dismisses
hybrid_threats.summary: Zelensky (30 Mar) proposed ceasefire for Orthodox Easter (16 April) and mutual halt on energy infrastructure strikes. Kremlin spokesperson Peskov dismissed proposal as lacking clarity. Ukraine's 'partners' signalled restraint on Russian energy strikes. Last year's Putin Easter ceasefire marred by violations.

hybrid_threats.headline: ISW: Russian Offensive Stalling — Internal Critics Admit Failure
hybrid_threats.summary: ISW Mar 30: Russian ultranationalist milblogger states forces 'unable to reverse unfavorable battlefield situation in coming months'; Ukrainian counterattacks 'rather successful.' Duma Defence Committee deputy questions Russia's ability to pursue offensive in 2026. Mixed battlefield: Russian advances near Hulyaipole/Luhivske but Ukrainian advances in Oleksandrivka (9+ settlements liberated), eastern Lyman. Largest strike series of war: nearly 1,000 drones/missiles Mar 23-24. Ukraine destroyed Zirkon launcher in Crimea.

institutional_developments.headline: EU-China Trade: Eurostat 2025 Data Confirms Widening Import Dependency
institutional_developments.summary: Eurostat (26 Mar): China remains EU's largest import partner at €559.4B (+6.4% YoY). EU overall trade surplus down to €128B (from €136B in 2024). EU-China goods deficit in €355-360B range. Commission rejected Merz suggestion for EU-China trade deal — demands Beijing address distortive practices first.

institutional_developments.headline: Slovenia: Parliament Inauguration 10 April, Coalition Talks Ongoing
institutional_developments.summary: Golob launched broad coalition talks (27 Mar). GS has 29 seats + allies = 40; SDS has 28 + allies = 43. Neither at 46-seat majority. SDS early voting complaint rejected. New parliament to be inaugurated 10 April. Seven parties won seats total.

institutional_developments.headline: Hungary: 12 Days to Election — Tisza Lead Widens in Independent Polls
institutional_developments.summary: Republikon (Mar 26): Tisza 49 vs Fidesz 40. PolitPro aggregate: Tisza 47.8% vs Fidesz 40.5%. Medián (Mar 20): Tisza 58 vs Fidesz 35 among decided voters. Government-aligned Nézőpont still shows Fidesz at 46 vs Tisza 40. Electoral system requires Tisza to win national vote by 3-5 points for parliamentary majority. Vance visit to Budapest announced for 'early April.'

institutional_developments.headline: €90B Ukraine Support Loan Blocked by Orbán at European Council
institutional_developments.summary: European Council (19-20 Mar): €90B Ukraine support loan for 2026-27 blocked by one leader (Orbán). Commission President von der Leyen: 'we will deliver one way or another.' Technical work on memorandum of understanding proceeding. Total EU support to Ukraine: €194.9B.

member_state_tracker.headline: Election 12 April — final campaign phase
member_state_tracker.change_from_last_week: Republikon poll shows Tisza lead widening to 49-40; Vance visit announced

member_state_tracker.headline: Parliament inaugurates 10 April; coalition talks ongoing
member_state_tracker.change_from_last_week: SDS complaints rejected; parliament inauguration date set; GS 40 seats vs SDS 43 (neither at 46 majority)

member_state_tracker.headline: SAFE €2.06B proposed; Pardubice false-flag probe deepens
member_state_tracker.status: ELEVATED — dual track (defence integration + capture risk)
member_state_tracker.change_from_last_week: SAFE second wave includes Czechia; intelligence probing Russian false-flag in Pardubice arson

member_state_tracker.headline: SAFE €15.09B proposed — largest single allocation
member_state_tracker.change_from_last_week: Commission proposed €15.09B SAFE allocation for France

flags.title: Hungary SDA Pre-Op + Alliance of Sovereign Nations
flags.description: The FIMI Monitor confirms Russia's Social Design Agency operating from the Budapest Embassy ahead of April 12 election (EEAS RAS activated 19 Mar) and tracks Turning Point Action's Alliance of Sovereign Nations as an assessed-active near-state US information operation. With 12 days to election, these converge on Hungary where Russian, US (MAGA-adjacent), and Israeli FIMI operations are simultaneously active.

flags.title: Hungary State Capture + Georgia Civil Society Collapse
flags.description: WDM Issue 3 (W/E 1 April) now tracking 29 countries, 15 in Rapid Decay. Hungary at Severity 5 with Tisza leading but electoral redistricting requiring 3-5pt margin. Georgia civil society capture architecture complete. Both feed into our state capture tracker — a Fidesz loss would materially alter P-EU-002.

flags.title: AI-Generated Election Content + EU AI Safety Independence Deficit
flags.description: AI Governance Monitor documents NewsGuard-confirmed AI-generated pro-Orbán TikTok content. Flags Armenia as high-probability FIMI target (June 2026). AI-enabled FIMI is a force multiplier; Digital Omnibus weakening of AI Act compounds vulnerability.

flags.title: Critical Mineral Dependencies + AMOC Early Warning
flags.description: Shared rare earth vulnerability tracking: 97% magnesium from China, 19/141 REE licences approved. AMOC third consecutive anomalous week at WATCH. Critical minerals are the shared structural condition between monitors.

flags.title: Hormuz Oil Premium + Russia Gasoline Export Halt
flags.description: Macro Monitor system stress ELEVATED→HIGH with $15-20 Hormuz premium. Russia's gasoline export halt (Apr-Jul) adds supply-side pressure. Intersects with our P-EU-003 energy score and Putin's strategic energy coercion.

flags.title: Sudan Displacement Cascade
flags.description: Sudan highest escalation signal (RSF Kurmuk, 73K displaced). Sahel-Horn instability is background migration pressure driver, historically instrumentalised by Russia and exploited in far-right narratives.

version_history.change: CMF-005 updated: added Russia gasoline export halt to Macro Monitor signal
